abstract: *Prangos ferulacea* is highly regarded in Iran for both its culinary flavor and its historical use in traditional medicine. This study aimed to characterize the phytochemical profile and biological activities of its extract, obtained via maceration. High-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) identified p-coumaric acid as the primary constituent, while Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR) confirmed the presence of functional groups associated with phenolics, flavonoids, tannins, and carboxylic acids. Quantitative analysis revealed a total phenolic content (TPC) of 202.04 ± 5.46 mg GAE/g, a total flavonoid content (TFC) of 1,909.46 ± 13 μg QE/g, and a beta-carotene concentration of 2.91 mg/100 g. The extract exhibited potent antioxidant activity, as evidenced by DPPH (IC50: 274 ± 7.2 μg/mL) and ABTS (IC50: 120.45 ± 9.6 μg/mL) radical scavenging assays, alongside a ferric reducing antioxidant power (FRAP) of 1.92 ± 0.05 μg AAE/g. Furthermore, the extract demonstrated significant cytotoxicity against HT-29 cancer cells, yielding an IC50 of 82.15 ± 0.02 μg/mL. Antimicrobial assays, including disk and well diffusion, MIC, and MBC, identified *Listeria monocytogenes* as the most susceptible pathogen, with MIC and MBC values of 16 and 128 mg/mL, respectively. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and confocal laser scanning microscopy (CLSM) revealed marked morphological damage to *L. monocytogenes* cells, while genetic analysis showed that the extract significantly downregulated transcripts responsible for biofilm formation. Collectively, these findings validate the therapeutic potential and functional versatility of *P. ferulacea* as a source of bioactive compounds.
